overall_complexity_rationale: 3 migrations for CatalogDB, 1 for IdentityDB. No stored procedures, triggers, or views. All changes are additive (schema creation) or corrective (column type/nullability fixes). No data migration scripts required.

migrations.name: 20201202111507_InitialModel
operations: CreateTable: Baskets (Id IDENTITY, BuyerId nvarchar(40) NOT NULL)
CreateTable: CatalogBrands (Id HiLo, Brand nvarchar(100) NOT NULL)
CreateTable: CatalogTypes (Id HiLo, Type nvarchar(100) NOT NULL)
CreateTable: Orders (Id IDENTITY, BuyerId nvarchar(max) nullable, OrderDate datetimeoffset, ShipToAddress_* nullable)
CreateTable: BasketItems (Id IDENTITY, FK → Baskets ON DELETE CASCADE)
CreateTable: Catalog (Id HiLo, FK → CatalogBrands CASCADE, FK → CatalogTypes CASCADE)
CreateTable: OrderItems (Id IDENTITY, FK → Orders ON DELETE RESTRICT)

migrations.name: 20211026175614_FixBuyerId
operations: AlterColumn: Orders.BuyerId nvarchar(max) nullable → nvarchar(256) NOT NULL (defaultValue: '')
migrations.reason: Original nvarchar(40) was too short for ASP.NET Identity email usernames (max 256). Orders.BuyerId was also made NOT NULL.
risks: Orders.BuyerId: If any existing row had a NULL BuyerId before migration, the NOT NULL constraint with defaultValue '' would silently replace the NULL with an empty string — not a hard error, but a data quality risk on existing data.
Baskets.BuyerId: Widening from nvarchar(40) to nvarchar(256) is safe on all RDBMS.

migrations.name: 20211231093753_FixShipToAddress
migrations.reason: ShipToAddress value object was initially created with nullable columns. FixShipToAddress enforced NOT NULL on the 4 required fields (State remains nullable — intentional for international addresses).
risks: Any pre-existing Orders with NULL address fields will have those columns replaced with empty string '' after migration. Not a SQL error but any such rows would have semantically empty addresses.

migrations.name: 20201202111612_InitialIdentityModel
operations: CreateTable: AspNetUserRoles (FK → AspNetRoles CASCADE + FK → AspNetUsers CASCADE)
CreateIndex: RoleNameIndex (unique, filtered: [NormalizedName] IS NOT NULL)
CreateIndex: UserNameIndex (unique, filtered: [NormalizedUserName] IS NOT NULL)

forward_migration_notes: All migrations are additive or corrective — no destructive DROP statements in Up() methods.
No cross-database migrations or coordinated ordering required between CatalogDB and IdentityDB.
HiLo sequences (catalog_hilo, catalog_brand_hilo, catalog_type_hilo) must exist before any INSERT into Catalog/CatalogBrands/CatalogTypes — created in InitialModel.
If migrating to a new DB engine (e.g. PostgreSQL), HiLo sequence strategy must be re-validated — behaviour differs by engine.

Buyer and PaymentMethod domain entities (BuyerAggregate) exist in code but have NO migrations — they are never persisted. Do not attempt to migrate them without first creating EF entity configurations and a new migration.
CatalogItem entity maps to table named 'Catalog' (not 'CatalogItems') — any raw SQL must use the correct table name.
